description_long: This chapel features a simple, rectangular structure with a pitched blue roof and a prominent cross mounted at the peak of the main gable. The exterior walls are a light gray or off-white, contrasting with the darker roof. The front facade includes a small, protruding entrance with a gabled roof and double doors, topped by a cross. Windows are minimal and rectangular, set symmetrically along the longer sides of the building. The rear section extends into a smaller, attached wing, maintaining the same roof and wall color scheme. The overall design is modest, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation, evoking a contemporary or vernacular chapel style.
description: A modest chapel with a blue pitched roof, light gray walls, and a cross atop the main gable. The structure features a small entrance porch and minimal rectangular windows.
description_view_0: The view shows the side and rear of the chapel, highlighting the blue roof and the cross at the roof's peak.
description_view_1: This top-down side view emphasizes the large blue roof and the attached rear wing, with the cross visible at the far end.
description_view_2: The front view displays the main entrance with a small gabled porch, double doors, and a cross above, as well as the symmetrical facade.
description_view_3: This rear-side view shows the long side of the chapel with several rectangular windows and the attached rear wing.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance features a small, gabled porch with double doors, likely made of wood and painted in a dark color. The design is simple, with no visible decorative elements beyond the cross above the entrance.
